title: [RFC] FSM dialect, FSM-to-HW conversion, and integration test of a target-AXI FSM
body:
This is a PR opened for discussion :smile:. It contains the following components:
1. An FSM dialect. This dialect is a target-agnostic representation of finite-state machine. It is designed to manipulate, query, and generate code in multiple domains, such as RTL hardware.
2. An FSM-to-HW conversion pass. We adopt an 1-always FSM coding style.
3. Integration test of the read channel of a target-AXI. Support burst read.
4. A simple DOT printer of the state graph.

To play with the FSM-to-HW lowering and verilog emission, run:
```sh
circt-opt integration_test/Dialect/FSM/target-axi-read.mlir -convert-fsm-to-hw \
-cse -canonicalize -prettify-verilog | circt-translate -export-verilog
```
